units.title: Work safety in livestock farm environment

learning_outcomes.description: Work safety in a livestock farm environment

performance_criteria.description: Explain safe work practices as obtained along the livestock production value chain
Identify safety signs and symbols on livestock farms
Provide correctly, the safety signs and symbols
Demonstrate safe work practices and instructions on livestock farms
Operate in accordance with health and safety practices

learning_outcomes.description: Comply with safety standards on livestock Farm.

performance_criteria.description: Identify work environment hazards
State types of hazards and risks associated with livestock farms
State safety standards on livestock farms and processing facilities
Use safety tools, materials and equipment in livestock processing facilities

learning_outcomes.description: Apply personal protective equipment (PPE) in livestock processing facilities

performance_criteria.description: Identify the types of PPEs required for livestock processing plants
Select appropriate PPE for livestock processing plants
Demonstrate the use of PPE
Maintain PPE before or after use

learning_outcomes.description: Response to accidents/injury in livestock processing plants

performance_criteria.description: Locate first aid facility
Use basic dressing materials
Respond to supervisor given instructions
Report incidences of accident/injury to the appropriate supervisor

learning_outcomes.description: Apply safe work habit and clean Ref. Page No. work environment in livestock processing plants

performance_criteria.description: Use safe access and exit routes in the work environment
Identify appropriate working tools, materials and equipment
Use tools and equipment safely in accordance with the supervisor’s instructions
Return all tools, equipment and unused materials for appropriate storage
Carry out general housekeeping of work environment
Dispose all wastes appropriately to designated waste facilities

learning_outcomes.description: Comply with standards of lifting, loading/offloading and stacking of materials in livestock processing facilities Ref. Page No.

performance_criteria.description: Identify lifting and stacking techniques
Demonstrate lifting techniques in loading and offloading of materials without assistance
Demonstrate correct lifting and loading techniques with mechanical assistance
Stack materials correctly

units.title: Effective communication in livestock Farm Environment

learning_outcomes.description: Apply the use of a non- complex communication system in a work environment

performance_criteria.description: Use a verbal means to pass on necessary information
Use non-verbal means to convey necessary information e.g.  body language,  signs
Interpret symbols and signs appropriately

learning_outcomes.description: Source for information on a livestock work environment

performance_criteria.description: Identify the source of information in the work environment
Relate effectively with the source of information
Use the different information flow systems in a work environment
Use information gathered to avoid challenges in a work situation
Report findings appropriately in accordance with laid down procedures in the work environment i.e.  Cards,  Flip Chart
Use simple communication gadgets like  mobile phones  table phones

learning_outcomes.description: Apply various means of communication in a livestock work environment

performance_criteria.description: Locate the various communication equipment in the work environment Use effectively the various communication equipment in a work environment
Pass information effectively to the right personnel
Obey instructions in line with ethics of the work environment

units.title: TEAM WORK IN LIVESTOCK FARM PRACTICE

learning_outcomes.description: Positive working relationship with colleagues

performance_criteria.description: Identify the need for developing positive working relationship with colleagues
Recognize the importance of relating with other people in a way that makes them feel valued and respected
Assist team members when required.
Report to the appropriate personnel when request for assistance fall outside area of responsibility.
Communicate information to colleagues about individual work that may affect team work.
Recognize own role and responsibilities within a team
Perform individual tasks in line with the team’s rules and regulations.
Participate effectively in teamwork.

learning_outcomes.description: Compliance with policy of organisation

performance_criteria.description: Explain organizational code of conduct
Work in line with organizational standard
Use organizational code of practice
Adhere strictly to instructions given by the Management

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the history and development of cattle, sheep and goat production

performance_criteria.description: Describe the various terminologies used in animal husbandry.
State the history and development of cattle, sheep and goat production.
Explain the socio-economic importance of cattle, sheep and goat production.
Explain factors that affect animal husbandry
Identify relevant laws and regulations applicable to cattle, sheep and goat production
Identify the various breeds of cattle, sheep and goats
Use a map of Nigeria to identify major cattle, sheep and goats producing areas.
Use map of Nigeria to locate the different breeds of cattle, sheep and goat within those areas.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the employment characteristics in cattle, sheep and goat production

performance_criteria.description: Outline the various career opportunities in cattle, sheep and goat production
Explain the value chain of cattle, sheep and goat production
List the key role players in cattle, sheep and goat production
Describe the main forms of marketing used in cattle, sheep and goat production

learning_outcomes.description: Understand work condition on a ruminant farm (LO) Ref. Page No.

performance_criteria.description: Carry out procedures involving machines and equipment with full operational safety as indicated by the manufacturer
Demonstrate restraining techniques in handling the animals
Identify the equipment used for personal protection on the farm (PPE)

units.title: Principles of calf, lamb and kid rearing

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the principles of calves, lambs and kids housing

performance_criteria.description: State the types of housing required for calves, lambs and kids
Identify the materials needed for the construction of each house
Identify and use the rearing equipment needed within each house

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the methods of feeding calves, lambs and kids

performance_criteria.description: Explain the nutrient requirements of calves, lambs and kids
Carryout feeding of calves, lambs and kids
State the importance of colostrum in calf, lamb and kid feeding

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the principles of calf, lamb and kid health management Ref. Page No.

performance_criteria.description: Identify the basic health challenges of calves, lambs and kids
List the steps to make calves, lambs and kids healthy
Carry out the routine vaccination for calves, lambs and kids
Identify the right age to wean calves, lambs and kids

units.title: Tools, equipment and materials in animal husbandry

learning_outcomes.description: Identify equipment, tools used in animal husbandry in Nigeria

performance_criteria.description: Identify the various tools, equipment and materials in animal husbandry
Use tools, equipment and materials in animal husbandry
Explain the maintenance practices of different equipment and tools in animal husbandry.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the equipment, maintenance and safety on livestock farm

performance_criteria.description: Demonstrate the use of tools, equipment and materials in animal husbandry
Demonstrate the maintenance and storage of tools, equipment and materials in accordance with manufacturer’s specifications.
Describe the safety precautions in the use of equipment and tools

learning_outcomes.description: Identify some consumables used in livestock farm in Nigeria Ref. Page No.

performance_criteria.description: List the consumables in animal husbandry
Use different consumables in animal husbandry
Carryout disposal of used/expired consumables in animal husbandry

learning_outcomes.description: Understand Ruminant nutrition

performance_criteria.description: Identify sources of concentrates and roughage
Highlight the nutritional contents of concentrates and roughage
List the different pasture (grasses) and legumes (pasture) used in feeding livestock
highlight the nutritional composition of grasses and legumes used in feeding livestock
Identify varieties of feed ingredients used in formulating livestock feeds
Identify the concentrates and roughages used in feeding livestock

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the techniques in formulating livestock feeds using locally available ingredients

performance_criteria.description: Describe the process of formulating feeds for the fattening of Ruminants
List the tools and equipment required to compound livestock feeds
Describe the equipment used in mixing livestock feeds
Demonstrate the techniques in mixing livestock feeds
Identify places where livestock feeds are compounded

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the different methods of processing and storage of livestock feeds Ref. Page No.

performance_criteria.description: State the benefits in processing livestock feeds in Nigeria
Describe equipment used in livestock feed processing
Explain methods of storage of feed raw materials and finished feeds products
Carryout the procedure involved in grinding grains for livestock feeding
Carryout the procedure involved in maintaining equipment for processing livestock feeds
Demonstrate how to make hay
Demonstrate how to store hay in a barn
Differentiate between feed raw materials and finished products

units.title: Common diseases and parasites of livestock

learning_outcomes.description: Understand common livestock diseases

performance_criteria.description: Identify the classes of livestock diseases (fungal, viral, bacterial, parasitic etc).
Identify signs of diseases in Ruminant farms
Identify the early signs of ill-health in animals:  Off feed.  Self-isolating.  Rough coat etc.
Identify diseased animals in the flock

learning_outcomes.description: Understand disease management and prevention

performance_criteria.description: Explain administration of drugs on diseased livestock
Identify the risk factors in treatment/handling of diseased animals
Carryout the preventive and control measures for the various types of animal diseases
Observe health and safety precautions in handling infected animals.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand common livestock parasites in animal husbandry

performance_criteria.description: Identify various livestock parasites
Carryout deworming, delousing, deticking of Ruminants parasitic diseases
Carryout prevention and control measures of major livestock diseases

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate understanding of preparing, maintaining and monitoring livestock accommodation

performance_criteria.description: Identify an ideal site for ruminant pen/house construction
Identify an ideal ruminant pen/house design based on location
Monitor the behaviour of individual animals for proper accommodation
Carryout sanitation in Ruminant pen/house
Identify and report faults in a Ruminant pen/house
Identify hazards in outdoor sites
Carryout tethering for the treatment of animals against diseases
Carryout dehorning to prevent injury to other animals

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the proper way to feed and water ruminant animals

performance_criteria.description: Describe the standard methods of feeding ruminants (feedlot, open grazing, supplementation)
Distinguish the normal feeding habits of the animals
Carryout hygiene measures for feeding and watering of animals
Carryout Feeding of right quantity of ration for animals in accordance with instructions
Provide water to the animals from good sources
Clean watering equipment before and after drinking and store equipment appropriately
Dispose feed remnants appropriately

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the proper way of restraining and transporting animals OBJECTIVE (LO)

performance_criteria.description: Identify tools and equipment for restraining of animals (rope snare, sedative,)
Carryout restraining of the animal for transportation.
Carryout restraining of the animal for treatment.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand biosecurity practices in livestock farms

performance_criteria.description: Identify measures for biosecurity in livestock farms (fence, trapping net, foot baths, quarantine of newly introduced animals etc
Set-up biosecurity measures at different points of the farm.
Carryout fumigation of transport vehicles, farm equipment and environment.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand biosecurity related to feeds and equipment

performance_criteria.description: Identify the source of contaminants in a feed mill (raw materials, equipment, finished feed etc)
Carry out stacking and storage of raw materials, finished feed and equipment in the proper order.
Fumigate the feed mill environment
Carryout receipt and issuance of raw materials and finished feed in accordance with FIFO
Confirm the source and history of new stock of animals into the farm (health, means of transportation etc)
Wear the appropriate PPEs

learning_outcomes.description: Understand types of biosecurity

performance_criteria.description: Demonstrate the quarantine of new animals to the livestock farm
Carryout isolation of sick animals
Assist, administer routine vaccines to farm animals
